title: Lesson 290
transcript: We've been talking about how we securely manage the requirements of the organization, which is what we call security management. We manage the security requirements of the organization in such a way so that we are always aligned and focusing towards and contributing to goals and objectives that are always focused on increasing value. One of the most important things that the security function needs to do is ensure that it does everything related to security based on due care and due diligence. Now, I know we've heard these words many times before. They're used in different contexts. but here it's important for us as security professionals to understand what due care and due diligence actually means from a security perspective. You may see some questions, that relate to what is the difference and what do these mean from a security perspective. So if you were to look up the word do care in a dictionary or the words do care in a dictionary, you might see something like it's doing what others would do in similar circumstances, but that doesn't really focus on what we're talking about here, which is security. So what we need to do is take that concept of due care and due diligence and apply it to security. What does it actually mean? Well, the perfect definition of due care is what you see up on your screen here. It is defined as the responsible protection of assets based on goals and objectives of the organization. And that's really important for us to understand and focus on. What it means is we are doing things related to security responsibly, but aligned with goals and objectives of the organization as we've said because that allow us to contribute and always focus on and be aligned with goals and objectives of the organization that are always focused on increasing value right so that's what do care means now They've used the word responsible protection of assets here. I happen to think that a better word would be the word accountable. As we've been saying, the accountable protection of assets based on goals and objectives would probably be the perfect definition of how do care relays or relates to security management however we've put the word responsible in here because that's probably what you're going to see and need to remember all right so do care responsible protection of assets based on goals and objectives What is due diligence? Well, due diligence can simply be summarized as being able to prove that we are doing the responsible protection of assets based on goals and objectives. In other words, proving it to the people that matter. And the people that matter are always gonna be your stakeholders. We've talked about that, but stakeholders are the important people that security really is contributing value towards. So that could be upper management, the CEO, the board of directors, but it could also be external stakeholders such as your shareholders, your clients and your customers, your regulators and possibly others that need to know on a regular basis that we are doing due care from a security perspective, responsible protection of assets. So just remember that responsible protection of assets is due care. Due diligence means being able to prove it to those that are mattering to us, which we refer to as our stakeholders.
